wiki.summary: "Heartless" is a song by American rapper Kanye West from his fourth studio album, 808s & Heartbreak, it was released as a promotional single 20 days before the release of 808s & Heartbreak, eventually being released as the second single from the album on October 28, 2008. The song was written by West, No I.D., Kid Cudi, Dekker Rowe, and Malik Yusef, while being produced by the former two. Musically, "Heartless" is an upbeat pop ballad, with hip hop and R&B influences and features synthesizers. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Heartless" is a song by American rapper Kanye West from his fourth studio album, 808s & Heartbreak, it was released as a promotional single 20 days before the release of 808s & Heartbreak, eventually being released as the second single from the album on October 28, 2008. The song was written by West, No I.D., Kid Cudi, Dekker Rowe, and Malik Yusef, while being produced by the former two. Musically, "Heartless" is an upbeat pop ballad, with hip hop and R&B influences and features synthesizers. The single's minimalistic artwork is the only art from the 808s era that doesn’t feature a heart motif; it’s literally heartless. Lyrically, "Heartless" features Kanye's forceful and direct reflection on breaking up with his ex-fiancé Alexis Phifer, expressing honesty and self-pity in the chorus, Kanye also asks a question angrily twice at the end of the chorus. Self-doubt is expressed on the song by Kanye, who raps that despite having "homies", he is ultimately "still so lonely". Kanye goes on to shed light on the post-breakup phase, assuming that Alexis will realize she is never going to find anybody better than him after a few months. It was passed on to Kanye during recording sessions, after originally being intended for inclusion on Jay-Z's eleventh studio album, The Blueprint 3 (2009). After Kanye's mother Donda West died, his mentor No I.D. was contacted by Malik Yusef, who asked him to spend time with the artist. No I.D. initially rejected the decision due to their differing personalities, though later began communicating with Kanye West more after persuasion from Malik Yusef. Kanye and No I.D. then travelled to Arkansas to work with Jay-Z on his eleventh studio album The Blueprint 3 (2009), before Kanye decided on transferring to recording for 808s & Heartbreak in the state once "Heartless" had been made. The two of them co-wrote it with Malik Yusef and fellow rapper Kid Cudi, was revealed to have penned the chorus in his documentary film A Man Named Scott (2021). In a November 2014 interview for the Juan Epstein show, No I.D. recalled that the song was intended for The Blueprint 3 until Kanye stopped during its recording and said, "No." Replying to him, No I.D. asked, "No what?"; Kanye insisted: "No way! This is my record!" No I.D. begged him to complete the song for the album, to which Kanye expressed assurance about it being for 808s & Heartbreak instead. Kanye premiered "Heartless" with a performance at the 2008 Democratic National Convention (DNC) in August, before he confirmed its release at a concert of rapper T.I. on October 5. A snippet of the song experienced an internet leak on October 12, 2008. Three days later, West posted a full unmastered version of the song on his blog for download and streaming, blogging that he had "no words". Alongside the post, he shared photographs of fuzzy faced, nude models that had been displayed at a listening party for 808s & Heartbreak on October 14, 2008. Outside of this song & Paranoid, Kanye does not rap at all on the album, though rap features are contributed to "Amazing" by Young Jeezy (or just "Jeezy") and "See You in My Nightmares" by Lil Wayne, respectively. In an interview with Kiss FM, Kanye revealed that Heartless was one of the first two tracks that he created once he went "into album mode" after the success of his third studio album Graduation (2007). The track's music video was directed and produced by Hype Williams, being set as a homage to Ralph Bakshi's film American Pop (1981); with Kanye admitting that inspiration was taken from the film after Williams showed it to him. For the video, Williams shot the footage of Kanye prior to the technique of rotoscoped animation being utilized. For the chart issue dated November 22, 2008, Heartless debuted the Billboard Hot 100 at #4, selling 201,000 downloads. It became Kanye's 3rd top-5 entry of 2008 alone following the posse cut "Swagga Like Us" with Jay Z, T.I. & Lil Wayne and "Love Lockdown", as well as the 12th song overall to debut in the top-4 of the Hot 100 in the 2000's decade thus far. It also was his second top-5 debut of the year as lead artist following the lead single "Love Lockdown" debuting at #3 the month earlier. After slipping out of the top-20 and rebounding into the top 10 at #7 on the week of December 27, 2008, the song re-peaked at #4 the next week before reaching #3 on the Hot 100 for the issue date of January 24, 2009, matching the peak of "Love Lockdown" and simultaneously standing as the fastest growing track at radio. The song peaked at #2 on the Hot 100 in February 2009, being blocked from the top spot by Eminem's "Crack a Bottle" featuring Dr. Dre & 50 Cent, which skyrocketed from #78 to the #1 spot. It became Kanye's fourth highest-charting song as a lead artist after 2004's "Slow Jamz" featuring Jamie Foxx & Twista, 2005's "Gold Digger" featuring Jamie Foxx & 2007's "Stronger" all of which reached #1.
